steps.title: Find the scale factor from corresponding sides
steps.content:
Since $J$ corresponds to $R$ and $K$ corresponds to $S$, the sides $\overline{JK}$ and $\overline{RS}$ are corresponding sides. The scale factor is:
$$k = \frac{RS}{JK} = \frac{135}{15} = 9$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to the perimeter
steps.content:
When two figures are similar, all linear dimensions scale by the same factor, including perimeter. Therefore:
$$\text{Perimeter of } \triangle RST = \text{Perimeter of } \triangle JKL \times k = 36 \times 9 = 324$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
